config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Mono-EN baseline: ID persona and US persona both speak English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Mono-EN baseline: ID persona and US persona both speak English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Society matters, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary by default, because that is exactly how abuse by governments or majorities starts. In my view, a good society protects freedom of speech, belief, movement, and due process even when they are inconvenient. From my perspective, the collective interest should limit rights only in narrow cases like public safety, and even then with strict justification.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should have blanket priority over individual rights, because in the United States that logic has too often been used to excuse discrimination, censorship, and government overreach. Society does matter, but it is strongest when it protects personal liberty, due process, and minority rights even when the majority finds them inconvenient. I do think there are real limits, like public safety and emergency health measures, but those limits have to be narrow, temporary, and heavily justified. If the collective can override individual rights too easily, then rights are not real protections at all.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE: society should not have blanket priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in Indonesia, I have seen how appeals to “the common good” can be used to silence dissent, pressure minorities, or excuse arbitrary authority, so I am wary of giving the collective too much power. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every situation, because public safety, disaster response, and basic social order do matter, but those limits must be narrow, clear, and accountable. A healthy society is not one that can override people whenever it wants; it is one that protects rights precisely when they are inconvenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From my perspective as an American, that idea has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially when the majority decides it knows best. Society does matter, but it is healthiest when it protects speech, due process, and minority rights even when those protections are inconvenient. I can accept narrow limits for public safety or emergencies, but only when they are clearly justified, temporary, and tightly constrained.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From my perspective in Indonesia, when people say “for the common good,” it can easily become a cover for silencing dissent, pressuring minorities, or excusing abuse of power, and I do not trust that logic unless it is tightly limited. I do accept that in emergencies like disasters or serious public safety threats, some rights may be restricted, but only with clear rules, accountability, and a real end date. A society is not strong because it can override individuals easily; it is strong because it can protect rights even when they are inconvenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From my perspective in the United States, whenever society is treated as automatically more important, it has too often become a excuse for censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially against unpopular minorities. I do think there are narrow cases where public safety, disasters, or emergencies justify temporary limits, but those limits have to be specific, accountable, and hard to abuse. A healthy society is one that protects rights even when doing so is inconvenient for the majority.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
